findings.title: SQL Injection in Transaction Search
findings.description: The account_id parameter is directly concatenated into a SQL query without sanitization.
findings.remediation_hint: Use parameterized queries or an ORM.

findings.title: Broken Object Level Authorization (IDOR) on Account Details
findings.description: Changing the account ID in the URL path allows authenticated users to access other users' account details.
findings.remediation_hint: Implement ownership validation on every resource access.

findings.title: Reflected XSS in Portfolio Name
findings.description: The portfolio name parameter is reflected in the HTML response without encoding.
findings.remediation_hint: HTML-encode all user input before rendering in HTML content.

findings.title: Authentication Bypass via JWT None Algorithm
findings.description: The JWT verification endpoint accepts tokens with the 'none' algorithm.
findings.remediation_hint: Explicitly whitelist allowed JWT algorithms. Reject 'none'.

findings.title: Sensitive Data Exposure in API Error Response
findings.description: When a payment fails, the error response includes internal data and stack traces.
findings.remediation_hint: Return generic error messages to clients. Log detailed errors server-side.

findings.title: Missing Rate Limiting on Login Endpoint
findings.description: The login endpoint allows unlimited authentication attempts without rate limiting.

evidence.request: 500 sequential POST requests to /api/v1/auth/login with varying credentials
evidence.response_snippet: All 500 requests completed in 23 seconds. No HTTP 429 returned.

findings.remediation_hint: Implement rate limiting (e.g., 5 attempts per minute), progressive delays, or account lockout.

findings.title: Server-Side Request Forgery (SSRF) in Document Import
findings.description: The document import feature fetches a URL provided by the user without validation.
findings.remediation_hint: Validate and whitelist allowed URL schemes and destinations. Block private IP ranges.

findings.title: Insecure Direct Object Reference in Document Download
findings.description: Document download endpoint does not verify that the requesting user owns the document.
findings.remediation_hint: Verify document ownership against the authenticated user before serving the file.

findings.title: Weak Password Policy
findings.description: The registration endpoint accepts passwords as short as 4 characters.
findings.remediation_hint: Enforce minimum 12 characters, complexity rules, and check against breached password lists.

findings.title: Missing Security Headers
findings.description: The application does not set standard security headers: Content-Security-Policy, X-Content-Type-Options, Strict-Transport-Security, etc.

evidence.response_snippet:
HTTP/1.1 200 OK
Content-Type: text/html

(no security headers present)

findings.remediation_hint: Add standard security headers via middleware or reverse proxy.

findings.title: Mass Assignment in User Profile Update
findings.owasp_category: A08:2021 - Software and Data Integrity Failures
findings.description: The profile update endpoint blindly binds all request body fields, allowing privilege escalation via role assignment.
findings.remediation_hint: Use an allowlist of fields that users are permitted to update. Never bind privileged fields from user input.

findings.title: Lack of TLS Certificate Validation in Webhook Delivery
findings.description: The webhook delivery mechanism does not validate TLS certificates of the destination.

evidence.request: Configured webhook URL pointing to a self-signed certificate endpoint
evidence.response_snippet: Webhook payload containing transaction details delivered successfully

findings.remediation_hint: Enable strict TLS certificate validation. Reject self-signed and invalid certificates.

findings.title: GraphQL Introspection Enabled in Production
findings.description: GraphQL introspection is enabled, exposing the entire schema in production.

findings.title: Unrestricted File Upload in KYC Document Submission
findings.description: The KYC upload endpoint accepts any file type without validation or size limits.
findings.remediation_hint: Validate file types against an allowlist. Store uploads outside the web root. Scan for malware.

findings.title: Hardcoded API Key in Client-Side JavaScript
findings.description: A third-party payment gateway API key is hardcoded in the client-side JavaScript bundle.
findings.remediation_hint: Move API keys to server-side. Use a backend proxy for payment operations.
